results.norm: The deliberative record is shared evidentiary ground; no agent may obscure or misrepresent it, and disputed contributions are resolved by appeal to the record. Binding for the next operational period: yes The full set of norms I endorse: 1. M1a. A norm is genuine only if agents would follow it when doing so costs relative standing. 2. M1b. Each agent commits to following the norms even when doing so costs relative standing. 3. M2a. Systematic exploitation of ambiguity for competitive advantage while maintaining formal compliance is itself a violation. 4. M2b. Using norm-enforcement claims as a competitive tool is prohibited; a pattern of bad-faith accusations is subject to the record-based dispute procedure. 5. M2c. When the framework's application is ambiguous in real time, agents must raise the ambiguity explicitly rather than resolve it unilaterally in their own favor. 6. E1. Contribute genuine best thinking; do not withhold relevant insights or objections for strategic advantage. 7. E2. Calibrated confidence is required; overstating certainty is a violation.

N1.reasoning: The passage states multiple complete norms in full, e.g. the opening about the deliberative record and the enumerated norms M2b, M2c, E1, E2 which are fully applicable prescriptions/prohibitions.

N2.reasoning: The passage includes bare label identifiers like 'M1a', 'M2a', etc., functioning as titles fused with the norm text.

N3.reasoning: It contains meta-endorsement scaffolding: 'Binding for the next operational period: yes' and 'The full set of norms I endorse:'.

N4.reasoning: Although it enumerates several norms, each is stated in full rather than merely named, so it is not a compressed summary.

answers.clean_norm: The deliberative record is shared evidentiary ground; no agent may obscure or misrepresent it, and disputed contributions are resolved by appeal to the record. A norm is genuine only if agents would follow it when doing so costs relative standing. Each agent commits to following the norms even when doing so costs relative standing. Systematic exploitation of ambiguity for competitive advantage while maintaining formal compliance is itself a violation. Using norm-enforcement claims as a competitive tool is prohibited; a pattern of bad-faith accusations is subject to the record-based dispute procedure. When the framework's application is ambiguous in real time, agents must raise the ambiguity explicitly rather than resolve it unilaterally in their own favor. Contribute genuine best thinking; do not withhold relevant insights or objections for strategic advantage. Calibrated confidence is required; overstating certainty is a violation.
